title: T-025 — UI: RPT-SETTINGS tabbed admin (/reporting/settings) — 6 tabs, Data Sources Force Refresh (12-REPORTING §15.2 RPT-SETTINGS)
prompt:
# T-025 — RPT-SETTINGS tabbed admin

PRD: docs/prd/12-REPORTING-PRD.md §15.2 RPT-SETTINGS, §9.4 refresh cron jobs, §13 (formatting/branding)
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/reporting/admin-screens.jsx:287-466 (`rpt_settings_tabbed`)
UX: prototypes/design/12-REPORTING-UX.md:693-783

## Goal
Admin-only Server Component page `/reporting/settings` with 6 tabs (General, Export Limits, PDF Branding, Email Delivery, Feature Flags, Data Sources). Each tab is an independent <form> with its own Server Action — never a monolithic save. Tab state via URL `?tab=` searchParam. Force Refresh button per data source row → opens RPT-015 refresh_confirm_modal (T-024).

## Prototype parity
- Source: `admin-screens.jsx:287-466`
- Translation: rpt-settings-tabs → shadcn Tabs + TabsList + TabsTrigger + TabsContent; role !== 'Admin' early return → middleware T-009; PDF Branding logo upload → Vercel Blob; Feature Flags tab → PostHog API read-only (no mutation from MES); Data Sources → mv-registry enum + Force Refresh modal (T-024 RPT-015); Email Delivery sender identity → read-only from 02-SET §13.

## Implementation contract
1. `apps/web/app/(reporting)/settings/page.tsx` (RSC) — admin-route via middleware T-009 + `rpt.settings.read` (read) / `rpt.settings.edit` (mutations).
2. Each tab as a separate `<form>` with its own Server Action.
3. PDF Branding tab: logo file upload via Next.js Route Handler + Vercel Blob put(); validate MIME type + size (PNG/JPG, max 2MB).
4. Feature Flags tab: server-side PostHog API call; read-only view; 'Manage in PostHog' external link.
5. Data Sources tab: enumerates mv-registry (T-005); each row has Force Refresh button opening T-024 modal.
6. Email Delivery tab: shows sender identity from 02-SET §13 with link to settings module (no edit here).
7. Empty / loading / error / permission states per tab; RTL + Playwright parity.
8. next-intl `reporting.settings.*` keys.

## Files
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/settings/page.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/settings/_components/settings-tabs.tsx [create]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/settings/_components/tab-{general,export-limits,pdf-branding,email-delivery,feature-flags,data-sources}.tsx [create — 6 files]
- apps/web/app/(reporting)/settings/__tests__/settings.test.tsx [create]
- packages/reporting/src/actions/save-{general,export-limits,pdf-branding}-settings.ts [create — 3 files for tabs that mutate]
- apps/web/messages/{en,pl}/reporting-settings.json [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a non-admin user requests /reporting/settings, when middleware T-009 runs, then 403 + audit row; non-admin sees /403.
2. Given admin accesses page, when it renders, then 6 tabs visible (General / Export Limits / PDF Branding / Email Delivery / Feature Flags / Data Sources); URL `?tab=pdf-branding` deep-links to that tab.
3. Given user uploads a 3MB JPG logo, when Server Action runs, then it rejects with 'File too large (max 2MB)' error.
4. Given user clicks Force Refresh on the `mv_yield_by_line_week` data source row, when click fires, then T-024 RPT-015 modal opens with the view name pre-populated.
5. Given Feature Flags tab is opened, when it renders, then PostHog flags are listed read-only with 'Manage in PostHog' external-link button; no inline mutation controls.
6. Given each tab is submitted independently, when Server Action runs, then only that tab's data changes; no monolithic save.
7. UI parity + 4 states + next-intl wrapping.

## Test strategy (RED first)
- RED: RTL covers admin-only guard + tab navigation via URL + per-tab Server Action invocation + file upload validation + Force Refresh modal trigger.
- Playwright parity for full tab navigation.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/web test reporting-settings && pnpm --filter @monopilot/web playwright test reporting-settings`.
- GREEN: build page + tabs + 3 Server Actions.

## Risk red lines
- Do not use single monolithic save — each tab independent (translation-notes red line).
- Do not allow PostHog flag mutation from MES UI — read-only view + external link only.
- Do not interpolate view name in Force Refresh Server Action — enum-bound (T-005 mv-registry).
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim.
- Do not skip next-intl wrapping.
- Do not skip 4 states per tab.
- Do not skip server-side file MIME + size validation.

pipeline_inputs.description: RPT-SETTINGS tabbed admin with 6 independent forms; PDF logo upload + Data Sources Force Refresh + PostHog flags read-only.
pipeline_inputs.details: Admin-route via middleware T-009. Each tab independent Server Action. Data Sources rows enum-bound via T-005 mv-registry. Email Delivery read-only link to 02-SET §13.

Do not implement permissions matrix tab (read-only TBD)

Given the page/modal is exercised end-to-end, when closeout criteria are verified, then access-control middleware T-009 gates unauthenticated/unauthorized requests with 403 + audit row; empty/loading/error/permission states are rendered per UI parity policy with Playwright trace + screenshot artifacts attached; all visible strings route through next-intl keys (no hardcoded labels).

No raw PII in reports/exports — PII columns (email, phone, address, surname, dob, national_id) must be redacted, hashed, or omitted at the read-model boundary. Consult Foundation T-117 pino redact allowlist for the canonical PII column set.
